title: Google’s new AI agent stack from I/O 2026
summary: Google I/O 2026 announced a four-layer AI agent stack including the Gemini 3.5 Flash model, Agent Development Kit 2.0, Agent2Agent Protocol, and Managed Agents API. The stack features graph-based orchestration, enterprise skill registries, and integrates the multimodal Gemini Omni Flash video model for production-ready agent deployment.
writeup: This video walks through the four-layer AI agent stack Google announced at I/O 2026, anchored by the Gemini 3.5 Flash model and Agent Development Kit 2.0. The stack covers interoperability via the Agent2Agent Protocol, a Managed Agents API, an agent CLI tool, and the multimodal Gemini Omni Flash model for video processing. It matters because Google is consolidating agent development into a defined layered architecture rather than scattered components. Related concepts include the Model Context Protocol, agent orchestration, test-time compute, and multimodal learning.
